oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Haptoglobin, milk yield, reproduction and herd removal: Evaluating the impact of calcium supplementation in early lactation dairy cows
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Poggi, Julia
Seminara, Jackson
Bach, Kathryn
McArt, Jessica
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Elevated inflammatory biomarkers early postpartum have been associated with negative downstream events, but treat­ments to reduce the impact of excessive inflammation remain lacking. Oral calcium supplementation is commonly used to improve lactational outcomes despite inconsistent research supporting its application. We evaluated whether oral calcium supplementation to multiparous dairy cows at 2 and 3 d in milk (DIM) would differentially impact outcomes of cows of varying inflammatory statuses at 2 DIM, assessed using serum hapto­globin concentrations. We hypothesized that cows with elevat­ed haptoglobin would have increased average daily milk yield through 15 wk of lactation, reduced time to pregnancy, and increased time to herd removal after calcium supplementation compared to less inflamed cows.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 249
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
